page: Amazon Web Services c6gd.large is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton2 processor using the 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz on the AWS Neoverse-N1 platform. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and is supported by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. A key hardware feature is the inclusion of 118 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates moderate single-core performance but shows limitations in multi-core and memory-intensive operations. The stress-ng single-core CPU test yielded an average score of 1519.49, while the multi-core CPU performance was weaker at 3013.99. Memory bandwidth tests showed 116.92 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 36.52 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, reflecting lower memory throughput. Cryptographic capabilities are supported via hardware-accelerated AES, though advanced vector extensions like AVX and AMX are absent.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c6gd.large offers a balanced entry point for ARM-based compute workloads requiring high-speed local NVMe storage without the overhead of larger instances. However, the low memory-to-vCPU ratio and poor multi-threaded decompression and database benchmark results represent a tradeoff for memory-bound applications. This server is best suited for lightweight compute tasks, static web serving, edge processing, and basic database operations that can leverage the local NVMe SSD.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.large is a compute-optimized, ARM64-based instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of memory, 118 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, and a baseline network speed of 1 Gbps. Benchmarks indicate average single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 1519.49, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain relatively weak. Lacking advanced vector extensions but supporting hardware AES cryptography, this instance offers a cost-effective profile for lightweight, storage-reliant ARM workloads. It is best suited for static web serving, basic database operations, and decentralized edge applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.large is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 118 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering average single-core performance for lightweight workloads.

tagline: An ARM-based, compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs
4.0 GB of system memory and 118 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Average single-core CPU performance with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Suited for static web serving, lightweight database tasks, and ARM development
